Summarise splits and lumps for a reconciliation object

Source: R/pr_splits_lumps.R
reconcile_splits_lumps.Rd

User-facing function that wraps pr_detect_splits_lumps() with formatted console output.

Usage

reconcile_splits_lumps(x, quiet = FALSE)

Arguments

x

A reconciliation object.

quiet

Logical. Suppress console output? Default FALSE.

Value

A list with $splits and $lumps tibbles, invisibly.

Examples

data(avonet_subset)
data(tree_jetz)
result <- reconcile_tree(avonet_subset, tree_jetz,
                         x_species = "Species1", authority = NULL)
#> ℹ Reconciling 919 data names vs 657 tree tips
#> ℹ Matching 919 x 657 names through 2 stages...
#> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching...
#> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (0 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (657 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (657 matched so far)...
#> ✔ Matched 657/919 data names to tree tips
sl <- reconcile_splits_lumps(result, quiet = TRUE)
sl$splits
#> # A tibble: 0 × 6
#> # ℹ 6 variables: name_resolved <chr>, names_x <list>, names_y <list>,
#> #   n_x <int>, n_y <int>, type <chr>
sl$lumps
#> # A tibble: 0 × 6
#> # ℹ 6 variables: name_resolved <chr>, names_x <list>, names_y <list>,
#> #   n_x <int>, n_y <int>, type <chr>
